# pfsense_certificate_authority (Resource)

Manages a [certificate authority](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/certificates/cas.html) (CA) in the pfSense trust store. Supports importing external CA certificates for TLS validation.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Import an external root CA certificate into pfSense
resource "pfsense_certificate_authority" "example" {
  descr       = "My Root CA"
  certificate = file("${path.module}/root-ca.crt")
  trust       = true
}

# Import a CA with private key for signing
resource "pfsense_certificate_authority" "signing_ca" {
  descr         = "Internal Signing CA"
  certificate   = file("${path.module}/signing-ca.crt")
  private_key   = file("${path.module}/signing-ca.key")
  trust         = true
  random_serial = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `descr` (String) Descriptive name for the certificate authority.
- `certificate` (String, Sensitive) PEM-encoded CA certificate.

### Optional

- `private_key` (String, Sensitive) PEM-encoded private key for the CA (optional, only needed for signing).
- `trust` (Boolean) Whether the CA is added to the operating system trust store. Defaults to `true`.
- `random_serial` (Boolean) Whether to use random serial numbers for issued certificates. Defaults to `false`.
- `next_serial` (Number) Next serial number to use when issuing certificates (sequential mode). Defaults to `0`.

### Read-Only

- `refid` (String) Unique reference ID assigned by pfSense.
- `subject` (String) Certificate subject (distinguished name).
- `issuer` (String) Certificate issuer (distinguished name).
- `serial` (String) Certificate serial number.
- `has_private_key` (Boolean) Whether the CA has a private key stored.
- `is_self_signed` (Boolean) Whether the CA certificate is self-signed.
- `valid_from` (String) Certificate validity start date.
- `valid_to` (String) Certificate validity end date.
- `in_use` (Boolean) Whether the CA is referenced by certificates, CRLs, or other configuration.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# Import an existing CA by its pfSense refid
terraform import pfsense_certificate_authority.example 69d6003269628
```
